Think about what information you need to find the complement. For each element `x`, you need to find `target - x`.
A brute force approach checks every pair — that's O(n²). Can you reduce the lookup of the complement to O(1)?
A HashMap can store the numbers you've already seen (value → index). For each new number, check if its complement already exists in the map.
Iterate once (single pass): for each index `i`, check if `target - nums[i]` is in your map. If yes, return. If no, add `nums[i]` to the map.
